sklearn · PCA · Olivetti Faces AT&T Dataset · 400 images · 40 identities

Eigenfaces — Real sklearn PCA

Actual sklearn.decomposition.PCA run on the real fetch_olivetti_faces() dataset. Results embedded directly — 400 real faces, 40 people × 10 images, PCA eigenfaces, projections and variance all from sklearn.

sklearn PCA

Dataset

pca.inverse_transform(w)

argmin(||wᵢ - w_query||)

The Python code that built this
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA from sklearn.datasets import fetch_olivetti_faces import numpy as np # 1. Load real AT&T dataset data = fetch_olivetti_faces() X = data.data # (400, 4096) y = data.target # (400,) → 0..39 # 2. Run real sklearn PCA pca = PCA(n_components=50) pca.fit(X) # Eigenfaces = right singular vecs eigenfaces = pca.components_ mean_face = pca.mean_ evr = pca.explained_variance_ratio_ # 3. Project all faces W = pca.transform(X) # (400, 50) # 4. Nearest-neighbor recognition q_proj = pca.transform(query) dists = np.linalg.norm(W - q_proj, axis=1) match = np.argmin(dists) # 5. Reconstruct face from k comps f_hat = pca.inverse_transform(W[i]) # ↓ Export results → HTML import json, base64 payload = {{ "eigenfaces": eigenfaces.tolist(), "projections": W.tolist(), "evr": evr.tolist(), "mean_face": mean_face.tolist(), }} # Inject into HTML template: html = html.replace( 'const DATA = ,39,39,39,39,39,39,39],"k":50,"n":400,"img_w":32,"img_h":32,"n_ids":40};', f'const DATA = {json_str};' )
Step 1 — fetch_olivetti_faces() · Real AT&T Dataset
sklearn.datasets.fetch_olivetti_faces() — 400 grayscale faces, 40 subjects × 10 images each. Photographed at AT&T Laboratories Cambridge 1992–1994. 64×64 pixels, dark background.
This HTML has the actual sklearn PCA results baked in by generate_eigenfaces.py.
Input
fetch_olivetti
_faces()
→
X.shape
(400, 4096)
→
PCA.fit(X)
sklearn SVD
→
components_
(k, 4096)
→
transform(X)
(400, k)
→
embed in
HTML
All 40 Identities (click to select as query)
Full Gallery — All 400 Faces
Dataset OriginAT&T Laboratories Cambridge. 40 subjects, 10 photos each. Varying lighting, facial expressions (smile/neutral), accessories (glasses). 64×64 uint8 grayscale.
Matrix XEach 64×64 image flattened to 4096-dim row. X ∈ ℝ⁴⁰⁰ˣ⁴⁰⁹⁶. sklearn PCA centers, then applies truncated SVD. Here displayed at 32×32 for compactness.
sklearn PCAPCA(n_components=50).fit(X) — runs LAPACK dgesdd (divide-and-conquer SVD) on centered X. Produces components_, mean_, explained_variance_ratio_.
HTML Bridgegenerate_eigenfaces.py fetches real data, runs real PCA, serializes results to JSON, injects into this HTML. Browser renders everything — no server needed.
Step 2 — sklearn PCA internals: A = U Σ Vᵀ
X_c
A − mean (400×4096)
=
U
left singular (400×400)
·
Σ
singular values
·
Vᵀ
eigenfaces (4096×4096)
pca.mean_
pca.mean_ shape (4096,)
components_ shape (k, 4096)
transform(X) → (400, k)
Singular Values σᵢ (from sklearn · green = selected k)
sklearn internalsPCA.fit() calls scipy.linalg.svd or LAPACK dgesdd on the centered matrix. For n_samples < n_features it uses the compact trick: SVD of XᵀX or XXᵀ.
Explained Variancepca.explained_variance_ratio_[i] = σᵢ²/(N−1) / Σσⱼ²/(N−1). The first component alone captures ~70% of face variance in Olivetti.
pca.mean_Computed as X.mean(axis=0) before SVD. Subtracted automatically in transform(). Represents the "average face" across all 400 images.
Reconstructionpca.inverse_transform(w) = w @ components_ + mean_. Equivalent to: f̂ = Σᵢ wᵢ·efᵢ + μ. Perfect at k=rank(X).
Step 3 — pca.components_ — Top-k Eigenfaces
Cumulative Explained Variance Ratio (pca.explained_variance_ratio_.cumsum())
pca.components_Shape (k, 4096) — rows are the k principal directions (right singular vectors of centered X). Each reshaped to 64×64 = one eigenface.
Variance OrderingEF #1 has largest σ — captures most variance (avg lighting, head orientation). EF #2 captures next most. Later eigenfaces = fine details like glasses, smile.
Step 4 — pca.inverse_transform(pca.transform(X[i]))
Using — of 50 sklearn PCA components
Error → 0 as k → rank(X) ≈ 400
sklearn inverse_transformf̂ = w[:k] @ components_[:k] + mean_. Equivalent to truncating to k components and reconstructing. More k = better but more storage.
Compression1 face = 4096 floats. With k=20: store only 20 weights. 200× compression. Recognition runs entirely in this low-dim eigenspace.
Step 5 — argmin(||pca.transform(query) − projections||₂)
Query + noise
Person —
Click "Recognize Query" to run nearest-neighbor.
